article_title: Repeal of the 4,500-Litre Minimum Production Requirement for Wineries

briefs.title: Wineries No Longer Required to Produce 4,500 Litres Annually to Keep Their Licence
briefs.summary: BC wineries are no longer required to produce a minimum of 4,500 litres per year to renew their manufacturer licence. Wineries are now treated the same as breweries and distilleries under BC's liquor licensing rules.
briefs.article_title: Repeal of the 4,500-Litre Minimum Production Requirement for Wineries
